page: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv4 server family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz, featuring 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs via Hyper-Threading. The system is equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 15.75 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage options.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across compute and memory subsystems. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is 1488.59, while multi-core performance reaches 29804.34. Memory bandwidth tests show 1661.23 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 142.18 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In database workloads, the server handles 27.94 million Redis SET operations per second and achieves a Passmark database operations score of 10989.83. LLM inference performance is average for smaller models but drops to weak or poor for larger models, reflecting the lack of hardware acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64-32s_v4 offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute allocation, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high local disk I/O. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces overhead for deployments relying on network-attached storage. This configuration is highly suited for large in-memory databases, enterprise applications, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs at 2.6 GHz, paired with 504.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.75 GB, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results place its compute and memory capabilities in the average tier, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 29804.34 and a large-block read memory bandwidth of 142.18 GB/sec. It demonstrates solid database performance, executing 27.94 million Redis SET operations per second. Given its high memory density and reliance on remote Premium Storage, the E64-32s_v4 is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale caching, in-memory databases, and enterprise application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 504 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, ideal for in-memory databases.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine designed for memory-intensive enterprise applications, caching layers, and large-scale database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
504.0 GB of RAM providing 15.75 GB per core
No local storage, optimized for remote Premium Storage
Delivers 27.94 million Redis SET operations per second
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and enterprise applications
